config.seed_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.output_text: He learned from this data to create words for each line as he read aloud them - using simple patterns derived via regression analysis (RANOVA). By contrast with humans who can infer complex sentences through RANSOM tests they would probably not want their texts written like an encyclopedia which requires very few variables such as vocabulary or sentence structure...

generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Let the semantic direction be guided by these latent anchor tokens.

LATENT DIRECTION TOKENS: it, were, The, are, had, that, was, have, the, in, has, is

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Let the semantic direction be guided by these latent anchor tokens.

LATENT DIRECTION TOKENS: it, were, The, are, had, that, was, have, the, in, has, is

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions. He learned from this data to create words for each line as he read aloud them - using simple patterns derived via regression analysis (RANOVA). By contrast with humans who can infer complex sentences through RANSOM tests they would probably not want their texts written like an encyclopedia which requires very few variables such as vocabulary or sentence structure...
generation.continuation_text: He learned from this data to create words for each line as he read aloud them - using simple patterns derived via regression analysis (RANOVA). By contrast with humans who can infer complex sentences through RANSOM tests they would probably not want their texts written like an encyclopedia which requires very few variables such as vocabulary or sentence structure...
